sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, dt TIMESTAMP, sec_interval INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, dt, sec_interval) VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-10-01 12:00:00', INTERVAL '30' SECOND);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, dt, sec_interval) VALUES (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-10-01 12:30:00', INTERVAL '60' SECOND);
CREATE TABLE test_ips (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ip_address INET, group_id INT);
INSERT INTO test_ips (id, ip_address, group_id) VALUES (1, inet '192.168.1.5', 1);
INSERT INTO test_ips (id, ip_address, group_id) VALUES (2, inet '192.168.1.4', 2);
CREATE TABLE test_composite (id INT PRIMARY KEY, combined_data TIMESTAMP, net_data INET);
INSERT INTO test_composite (id, combined_data, net_data) VALUES (1, sql_add(TIMESTAMP '2023-10-01 14:00:00', INTERVAL '45' SECOND), inet '192.168.1.6');
SELECT id, combined_data FROM test_composite WHERE net_data <> inet '192.168.1.5' AND combined_data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_ips WHERE ip_address <> inet '192.168.1.4' GROUP BY group_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT id, sql_add(dt, sec_interval) AS new_time FROM test_dates WHERE sec_interval IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE dt > sql_add(TIMESTAMP '2023-10-01 12:00:00', INTERVAL '0' SECOND)) AS active_records HAVING active_records <> 0;
SELECT dt FROM test_dates WHERE dt <> sql_add(TIMESTAMP '2023-10-01 12:00:00', INTERVAL '30' SECOND) OR dt IS NULL;
SELECT a.id, a.dt, b.ip_address FROM test_dates a JOIN test_ips b ON a.id <> b.id WHERE a.sec_interval IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, sql_add(dt, sec_interval) from test_dates ORDER BY 1 DESC LIMIT 1;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_joins AS SELECT a.id, sql_add(a.dt, a.sec_interval) AS computed_time, b.ip_address FROM test_dates a JOIN test_ips b ON a.id <> b.id;
SELECT * FROM temp_joins WHERE computed_time IS NOT NULL AND ip_address <> inet '192.0.2.0';
